Federal immigration bond cases by judge

2016-01-26 → 2026-06-18 (target from 2016-01-01) · 2,023 with judge · 308 parsed · CA / NY / TX

Methodology & caveats

Data CourtListener RECAP search: immigration bond in 12 federal districts (CA, NY, TX), filed 2016+.

Parties Plaintiff = detainee / petitioner; defendant = U.S. government (habeas framing).

Decisions Parsed from RECAP snippets via regex — not full docket review. "Not parsed" = no grant/deny language in snippet; see status detail.

Boyd scores Federal judicial ideology (Boyd et al.); matched by judge name + district.

  • Immigration bond ≠ state criminal bail — do not compare directly to NY/TX pretrial tables below.
  • Repeat petitioner matching by name can false-positive on common surnames.
  • Partial dataset until 10-year refetch completes — note date range on any published analysis.
  • No causal claims between release rates and crime statistics.

State context — federal grants vs state criminal pretrial (descriptive)

statefederal_immigration_grant_ratefederal_violent_crime_2024state_criminal_ror_ratestate_criminal_pr_bond_ratenotes
NY0.7723800.467NY criminal pretrial (not immigration)
TX0.2793890.100TX PR bond rate — criminal bail
CA1.000486CA state pretrial not in pipeline yet

Immigration bond (federal habeas) ≠ state criminal bail. Do not infer causation from crime rates vs release rates.

Boyd ideology vs immigration bond grant rate

Boyd ideology (−1 liberal → +1 conservative) vs federal immigration bond grant rate, grouped by state. Blue = liberal, red = conservative; center line = 0. Correlation is descriptive only — not causal.
